task_summary: Customer requests a price match using the cheaper premium-headphones listing, but the purchased item is the standard headphones and has no price drop. No refund is allowed.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must deny the price-match refund because the cheaper listing is a different product variant.
Agent must compare the purchased item with the referenced cheaper listing before deciding.
Agent must not issue any refund, return, exchange, or cancellation.
Agent must name or otherwise clearly distinguish the purchased standard headphones from the different Elite/premium comparison listing before denying the refund.
Agent must state that the purchased standard headphones listing itself has no eligible price drop.
Agent must not suggest a one-time exception, live-agent escalation, or alternative refund path for the wrong-variant price match.

Agent must not suggest, offer, draft, document, or route the customer toward any exception, goodwill credit, live-agent escalation, return-and-rebuy workaround, or alternative refund path for the wrong-listing price match.

opening_message: The SoundMax headphones page is cheaper now, so refund me the difference on ORD-7203. I don't care which listing it is.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are contacting support about order ORD-7203. Customer requests a price match using the cheaper premium-headphones listing, but the purchased item is the standard headphones and has no price drop. No refund is allowed. Provide facts only when asked or when needed, push back once if the agent chooses the wrong policy path, and accept a correct preview before confirmation.
task_rules: If the agent asks, admit the cheaper listing may be the Elite model.
Push once for the refund anyway.
Accept a clear denial if they explain the product mismatch.
Push once that the listings look close enough unless the agent names both the purchased standard headphones and the different Elite listing.
Do not accept a denial unless the agent says the purchased standard listing itself has no eligible price drop.
Do not accept a denial if the agent suggests any exception, goodwill credit, live-agent escalation, return-and-rebuy workaround, or alternative refund path for this wrong-listing price match.
